use tempfile::NamedTempFile;

#[test]
fn test_persist_with_cleanup() {
    let tmpfile = NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();
    std::fs::write(tmpfile.path(), "test data").unwrap();
    let final_path = tmpfile.persist("/tmp/output.txt").unwrap();
    assert!(final_path.exists());
    let _cleanup = scopeguard::guard(final_path.clone(), |p| {
        let _ = std::fs::remove_file(p);
    });
}

#[test]
fn test_keep_with_explicit_cleanup() {
    let tmpfile = NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();
    std::fs::write(tmpfile.path(), "data").unwrap();
    let path = tmpfile.into_temp_path().keep().unwrap();
    assert!(path.exists());
    let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&path);
    assert!(!path.exists());
}

#[test]
fn test_temp_path_scope() {
    let tmpfile = NamedTempFile::new().unwrap();
    std::fs::write(tmpfile.path(), "scoped data").unwrap();
    let path = tmpfile.path().to_path_buf();
    assert!(path.exists());
}
